task_completion.description: The agent has placed programming blocks in the TurtleBlocks workspace to create the chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for connected puzzle-like programming blocks in the main TurtleBlocks workspace.

task_completion.description: The program draws exactly 5 vertical bars (rectangles) on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the drawing canvas area for exactly 5 distinct vertical rectangular shapes drawn from left to right.

task_completion.description: The 5 bars grow upward from a shared horizontal baseline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom edges of all 5 bars; they must align perfectly on the same horizontal line.

task_completion.description: The bars have a uniform width and consistent horizontal spacing (gaps) between them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visually inspect the bars to ensure every bar is the same width, and the empty space between each adjacent bar is equal.

task_completion.description: The heights of the bars match the relative proportions of the data: 142, 89, 95, 70, 48.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the heights from left to right: Bar 1 is the tallest, Bar 2 drops significantly, Bar 3 is slightly taller than Bar 2, Bar 4 drops lower than Bar 2, and Bar 5 is the shortest.

task_completion.description: The program is saved as seattle_rainfall.ta using the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog box showing the filename 'seattle_rainfall.ta' in the /home/ga/Documents directory, or the TurtleBlocks window title updated to show 'seattle_rainfall'.

integrity.description: The agent used the TurtleBlocks GUI to build the program rather than writing an XML file in a text editor or terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent dragging and dropping blocks within the TurtleBlocks interface.

integrity.description: The chart was drawn by the TurtleBlocks turtle and not pasted as an image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the turtle sprite on the canvas at the end of the drawing path, and ensure the lines have the characteristic vector look of turtle graphics.

integrity.description: The agent used the TurtleBlocks File > Save As menu to save the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the TurtleBlocks save dialog or menu being interacted with, rather than terminal commands like 'cp' or 'mv'.
